seoTitle: How to Edit a Canva Website After Publishing
metaDescription: Learn how to edit your Canva website after it's already published, so you can update content without starting from scratch.
intro: Websites need regular updates, and Canva makes it possible to go back and edit a site even after it's live, without losing your published version until you republish. This guide covers the process of making and pushing those changes.

steps.heading: Open your existing website project
steps.body: Go to your Canva projects and select the website you previously published.

steps.heading: Make your edits in the website builder
steps.body: Update text, images, or layout sections just as you did when first building the site.

steps.heading: Preview your changes
steps.body: Use the preview mode to check how the updated site looks before making it live.

steps.heading: Republish the site
steps.body: Click Publish again so your edits go live and replace the previous version.

steps.heading: Confirm the live site reflects your updates
steps.body: Visit your site's URL after publishing to make sure the changes appear correctly.

faq.question: Do I lose my published site while I'm editing it?
faq.answer: No, your live site stays up as-is until you choose to republish with the new changes.

faq.question: Can I edit a Canva website from mobile?
faq.answer: Editing is best done on desktop for more precise control, though basic changes may be possible on mobile.

faq.question: Will my custom domain stay connected after republishing?
faq.answer: Yes, republishing updates the content on your existing domain without needing to reconnect it.

faq.question: Is editing a published site free?
faq.answer: Yes, editing is included, though certain website features like custom domains may require a paid plan.
